title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 6 RECAP
content: Week 6 was one for the books. Black's undefeated run came to an end in a gut-wrenching overtime loss to Orange, with Stefan Radan pouring in 43 points and 23 rebounds in a shorthanded effort. Green kept rolling with a 117-89 blowout of White. Yellow found its footing against Pink, Purple survived a scrappy Tan team, and Gray took care of Red behind a balanced effort. Let's get into the action.

games.text: Black's perfect season ended in overtime, but it was not for lack of trying. Orange jumped on Black early and built a 14-point lead heading into the break. Trent Roleau was the story of the first half, scoring at will in the paint and bullying the competition. Cole Sampson added 17 points, while Will Oboh made plays on both ends. Halftime: Orange 48, Black 34. Black needed everything they had, and Radan gave it to them. Radan took complete control in the second half. He finished with 43 points on 16-of-36 shooting to go with 23 rebounds and 5 assists. Cyle Chappy went 5-of-11 from three for 15 points, and Tanner Freeman hit massive shots down the stretch to keep it close and force overtime. In overtime, Orange edged Black 7-6 to hand the previously unbeaten squad their first loss of the season. Orange won, 89-88 (OT).

performers.Black: Stefan Radan — 43 pts, 23 REB, 5 AST; Cyle Chappy — 15 pts, 1 REB, 1 AST; Tanner Freeman — 12 pts, 4 REB, 4 AST
performers.Orange: Trent Roleau — 41 pts, 12 REB, 1 AST; Cole Sampson — 17 pts, 9 REB, 3 AST; Will Oboh — 12 pts, 9 REB, 7 AST

games.text: Yellow needed a bounce-back win, and they got one convincingly. Yellow came out with purpose and built a 17-point lead before the break. Drew Pelkey led the way with efficient scoring from multiple levels, and Cam Perta was hot from three. Logan Letourneau may not have filled up the stat sheet offensively, but his 9 assists and 10 rebounds kept Yellow's offense clicking. Pink's Kyle Haley and Tyrone Conley were both unavailable, leaving a significant gap in their lineup. Halftime: Yellow 40, Pink 23. Pink needed a dramatic second half, but it never materialized. Yellow continued to apply pressure in the second half, and Liam Walsh did everything in his power to keep Pink afloat. He finished with 22 points and 18 rebounds. Max Taylor chipped in 11 points. But Yellow's lead was too large and too well-earned. Yellow won, 77-48.

performers.Yellow: Drew Pelkey — 21 pts, 2 REB; Alex Philo — 16 pts, 7 REB, 3 AST; Cam Perta — 14 pts, 1 REB, 1 AST
performers.Pink: Liam Walsh — 22 pts, 18 REB, 1 AST; Max Taylor — 11 pts, 8 REB; Aid Semic — 7 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST

games.text: Tan made this one interesting, but Purple had just enough to hold on. Purple controlled the first half and took a comfortable 12-point lead into the break. Trevis Buckhannon was his usual dominant self on the glass, and Jimmy Warden provided steady offense and playmaking. Tan refused to go away and played their most competitive half of the season, but the Purple lead felt secure at the break. Halftime: Purple 39, Tan 27. Purple kept it steady in the second, but slowly faded. This allowed Tan to take advantage. Hakeem Faud led Tan with 24 points on 9-of-20 shooting, while Aiden Brown, Brody Snow, and Devin Bradford all contributed double digits. Tan showed real fight and nearly pulled off the upset, as they closed the gap to single digits. But Brandon Huestis kept the outside game honest, drilling timely three-pointers, and Buckhannon delivered in the clutch, finishing with 20 points and 19 rebounds. Purple held on for the win, 74-63.

performers.Purple: Jimmy Warden — 21 pts, 5 REB, 3 AST; Trevis Buckhannon — 20 pts, 19 REB; Brandon Huestis — 19 pts, 3 REB, 2 AST
performers.Tan: Hakeem Faud — 24 pts, 9 REB, 3 AST; Brody Snow — 11 pts, 3 REB, 2 AST; Devin Bradford — 11 pts, 5 REB, 1 AST

games.text: Green sent a message to the rest of the league: they are not slowing down. Green came out with their most complete performance of the season. Asko Mostarlic was exceptional, finishing with 27 points on 10-of-22 shooting with 5 threes, 9 rebounds, and 8 assists. Mahlik Franklin was an absolute nightmare for White's defense, going 12-of-14 from the field for 27 points, and Andy Bousono added 22 points and 7 assists. White had no answer for Green's depth and shooting, and fell behind by 16 at halftime. Halftime: Green 55, White 39. White tried to mount a comeback in the second half, but Green was relentless. Zaire Wilson fought hard with 25 points and 11 rebounds, and John Helsel was tremendous with 24 points and 15 rebounds. Matt Riordan also added 23 points and 11 rebounds of his own. It was wild that White had three players go for over 20 and still lost by 28. That is how good Green was on this day. Green won, 117-89.

performers.Green: Asko Mostarlic — 27 pts, 9 REB, 8 AST; Mahlik Franklin — 27 pts, 6 REB, 5 AST; Andy Bousono — 22 pts, 5 REB, 7 AST
performers.White: Zaire Wilson — 25 pts, 11 REB, 4 AST; John Helsel — 24 pts, 15 REB; Matt Riordan — 23 pts, 11 REB, 3 AST

games.text: Gray controlled this one from start to finish and got a balanced effort to pick up the win. Gray built a six-point lead at halftime behind Manny Robertson, who was aggressive and productive, while Kevin Garrison dominated the glass. Red was shorthanded but battled hard. Peter Walsh came off the sub list for Red and provided a significant boost, doing damage in the paint and stretching the defense with his scoring. Halftime: Gray 27, Red 21. Red needed a big second half, but Gray stayed disciplined. Gray won the second half 34-32 and held on comfortably. Robertson finished as Gray's leading scorer with 19 points on 8-of-14 shooting. Garrison had another big rebounding game with 16 boards to go with 12 points and 8 assists. For Red, Walsh finished with 24 points and 9 rebounds in a strong showing, and Logan Tobin added 14 points. Meanwhile, Will Ewald grabbed a ridiculous 19 rebounds. But Gray's collective effort was too much. Gray won, 61-53.

performers.Gray: Manny Robertson — 19 pts, 7 REB; Kevin Garrison — 12 pts, 16 REB, 8 AST; Kyle DeCoster — 12 pts, 8 REB
performers.Red: Peter Walsh — 24 pts, 9 REB; Logan Tobin — 14 pts, 7 REB, 1 AST; Will Ewald — 7 pts, 19 REB, 3 AST

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: This recap is proudly brought to you by the Alta Group and The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub. Thank you for your continued support of the Revolution Men's League.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is also brought to you by Warden's Wisdom — a mindfulness-based coaching business dedicated to helping individuals relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices. Whether you're fighting back from 14 down or trying to stay steady at the top of the standings, a grounded mind is always the edge.
sponsors.quote: You don't rise to the level of your talent. You fall to the level of your preparation.

closing: Green stays perfect. Black and Purple stay dangerous. Week 7 is coming. Tune in.
